arts.ttl: I never wanted to leave Newcastle United for Aston Villa but was Player of the Year months later
arts.sum: For many Newcastle United fans, the name Nobby Solano instantly transports them back nearly twenty years to a different Premier League where players could show off their personality and flair. The move came to fruition after the winger began to fall out of favour under Sir Bobby Robson, and with an out-of-position Lee Bowyer favoured out wide he knew his days were numbered.

arts.ttl: “Eddie Howe would be happy” – Newcastle one of few clubs that can afford Juventus star
arts.sum: Newcastle United are one of the few sides that can afford the wages of Juventus man Dusan Vlahovic, according to Italian media, while Eddie Howe would be “very happy” with his signing. arts.ttl: Aston Villa transfer news: Atletico Madrid 'circling' for midfielder, facing La Liga competition
arts.sum: Atletico Madrid are reportedly among a number of clubs who are contemplating whether to make an approach for an Aston Villa midfielder during the summer transfer window.

arts.ttl: Chelsea and Newcastle make contact with Bayern defender
arts.sum: Chelsea and Newcastle United have both spoken to the representatives of Bayern Munich defender Kim Min-jae, who is open to leaving the German side this summer. Bayern Munich are willing to listen to offers for the South Korean centre-back, following a mixed time with the club. Juventus are also admirers of Min-jae, who excelled at […] The post Chelsea and Newcastle make contact with Bayern defender first appeared on The Football Faithful.

arts.ttl: How 'sensational' Aston Villa surprised pundit as concern lingers for clash against 'monsters'
arts.sum: It's a big evening in the Champions League race at Villa Park as Aston Villa take on Newcastle, with Chris Sutton and Paul Merson running the rule over the in-form sides Villa go into the game (5.30pm kick-off) off the back of four straight Premier League victories and following their superb effort against PSG in the Champions League quarter-finals in midweek, Unai Emery’s men going close to one of the all-time great European fightbacks.

arts.ttl: 'Wow' - Unai Emery reveals Jason Tindall and Newcastle United feelings after Aston Villa brawl - Chronicle Live
arts.sum: However, Emery has tried to play down the bust-up by saying he has no issue with the Newcastle coaching team while offering his thoughts to Howe and his recovery. Aston Villa boss Unai Emery says he has "no problem" with Newcastle United's Jason Tindall after the two men were involved in a huge brawl during the last meeting between Aston Villa and the Magpies on Boxing Day.

arts.ttl: Newcastle XI vs Aston Villa – Predicted lineup and team news
arts.sum: Newcastle United head to Aston Villa in the Premier League this evening with both teams chasing Champions League football. The Magpies are targeting a seventh straight win in all competitions. Despite the absence of manager Eddie Howe through illness, Newcastle have thrashed Manchester United and Crystal Palace over the last week to climb to third […] The post Newcastle XI vs Aston Villa – Predicted lineup and team news first appeared on The Football Faithful.

arts.ttl: Newcastle United see £32m training ground possibilities as upgrade plans announced
arts.sum: Newcastle United this week announced grand plans to extend their current Benton training facility as the search continues for a brand new location in the future. Newcastle's announcement comes weeks after Bournemouth unveiled their new surroundings, years in the making.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa vs Newcastle – Predicted lineup and team news
arts.sum: Aston Villa host Newcastle United in the Premier League this evening in a huge clash at Villa Park. Both teams are in form and chasing a place in next season’s Champions League. Villa have won nine of their last 10 games in all competitions and beat Paris Saint-Germain 3-2 in Europe in midweek. Though the […] The post Aston Villa vs Newcastle – Predicted lineup and team news first appeared on The Football Faithful.

arts.ttl: McGinn: We’ve made Villa Park tough place to come
arts.sum: Fixtures Aston Villa Vs. Newcastle United - Sat, Apr 19th 2025, 17:30 John McGinn is hoping Villa Park proves a fortress for he and his team-mates once again as Aston Villa prepare to take on Newcastle United on Saturday evening. The Villans welcome the Magpies to B6 looking to further close the gap on the Champions League spots of the Premier League table, while aiming to extend their 18-match unbeaten run on home soil in all competitions. McGinn was spurred on by the incredible support from the stands in Tuesday’s Champions League quarter-final second leg tie against PSG on Tuesday, and believes the home supporters can again play a big part in this weekend's "six-pointer". He said: “It gives us massive confidence. We’re really strong at home. We’ve made it a tough place to come, especially over the past few seasons. “But Newcastle are playing really well. They’re strong, they’re a tough team to play against. “I think our record against them at Villa Park over the past few seasons is strong, and they hammered us at St James’ Park earlier this season. “We know how important the game is. They’re in probably their best run of the season since winning the cup, so it’s a huge game for both teams. “They’re obviously five points ahead of us but it’s our job to try and close that gap and stay on their toes. “Definitely, Saturday is a massive game, a six-pointer really.” Reflecting further now the dust has settled on Villa’s Champions League exit, the skipper reiterated his pride in how the side conducted themselves during a first campaign in Europe’s elite competition for 42 years. The Scotland international felt the Paris outfit came up against the full force of Villa Park and how difficult a place it can be to visit, and hopes the positives can drive Unai Emery’s side forward further for the remainder of the season. The 30-year-old added: “Looking back over the campaign, we can be delighted. “We felt we had more, we felt we could have progressed to the semi-final, but over the season, we put in a really good performance. “I think, for us, it was to put ourselves back on the map in European football, and compete with the best teams and best players, and I think we managed to do that. “I think PSG were a little bit shocked and surprised. After they went 2-0 up, I think they thought they were going to have an easy night. “But credit to the lads. We dug deep, and we proved that Villa Park is a really tough place to come.”
